Member (Judicial-V), Board of Revenue / Chief Settlement Commissioner etc. Ch. Muhammad Iqbal, J:- Through this single judgment, I intend to decide the titled Writ Petition [No.12451/04/2017] as well as following petitions, as common questions of law and facts are involved in all these case: Date of hearing: 11.03.2022, 22.03.2022, 24.03.2022 & 25.03.2022 Petitioners by: Sardar Qamar Sultan, Barrister Haroon Duggal, Ahmad Raza, Advocates for petitioners in W.P.No.89-R/2012 & W.P.No.12451/04/2017 Mian Rafaqat Ali, Advocate for petitioner in W.P.No.10498-R/2017 Mr. Ameer Hamza Dogar and Ahmad Danish, Advocates for the petitioner in W.P.No.12469/2017 Malik Noor Muhammad Awan, Advocate for the petitioner in W.P.No.13392/04/2017 Mr. Ghazanfar Khalid Saeed and Sohail Arshad Warraich, Rashid Yamin Kazmi, Advocates for the petitioners in W.P.No.14058/2017 & W.P.No.12081/04/2017 Mr. Muhammad Shahzad Shaukat, Advocate for the petitioners in W.P.No.55606/2017 Malikzada Hameed-ur-Rehman Khokhar, Advocate for the petitioner in W.P.No.69194/2017 Ch. Nemo for the petitioner in W.P.No.58738/2019 Respondents by: Mr. Asif Mehmood Cheema, Additional Advocate General Mr. Waseem Iqbal Butt and Riaz Ahmad Shad, Advocates for Settlement Department Hafiz Abdul Rehman Shahid, Advocate for P.H.A Sahibzada Muzaffar Ali and Irfan Haider Goraya, Advocates for L.D.A.

No.89-R/2012 & W.P.No.12451/04/2017 2. Brief facts of these cases, as stated in the writ petitions, are that Col. (R) Hamid Hassan Khan [hereinafter referred to as the “alleged allottee”] was a displaced person, who allegedly having verified claim, obtained land measuring 75 Kanal in Moza Ichraa, Lahore by the Chief Settlement & Rehabilitation Commissioner against Khata RL.II. No.42 dated 04.04.1961. From the above alleged quantum of land, the alleged allottee sold out land measuring 51 Kanals 17 Marlas 81 Sq. Ft. to different vendees, (the petitioners in other connected writ petitions). After the death of alleged allottee, the land measuring 30 Kanals 09 Marlas 132 Sq. Ft. was devolved upon his legal heirs including Mst.Shah Khanum (daughter) through inheritance mutation No.25841 dated 25.05.1991. Lahore Improvement Trust succeeded by Lahore Development Authority [hereinafter referred to as the “L.D.A.”] acquired the said land for establishment of New Garden Town Scheme, Lahore in 1969 and awarded different exempted plots in lieu of acquired land to the ostensible owners. That on disclosure of some fraud qua the suit land the petitioner [Shah Khanum] approached the Chief Minister Inspection Team which referred the matter to the Anti-Corruption Establishment, Punjab.

Lahore Improvement Trust succeeded by Lahore Development Authority [hereinafter referred to as the “L.D.A.”] acquired the said land for establishment of New Garden Town Scheme, Lahore in 1969 and awarded different exempted plots in lieu of acquired land to the ostensible owners. That on disclosure of some fraud qua the suit land the petitioner [Shah Khanum] approached the Chief Minister Inspection Team which referred the matter to the Anti-Corruption Establishment, Punjab. The matter was then referred to Chief Settlement Commissioner, Punjab, who, after probing the record, Writ Petition No.12451/04/2017 3 declared the alleged allotment at Khata RL.II.42 in the name of so-called allottee Col. (R) Hamid Hassan Khan, as bogus and fictitious vide order dated 09.02.2009. This order was challenged by the petitioner through Writ Petitions [No.64-R/2009 & No.79- R/2009] which were allowed by this Court, by setting aside the order dated 09.02.2009, remanded the case to the Chief Settlement Commissioner for fresh decision. In post-remand proceedings, the Chief Settlement Commissioner, Punjab vide order dated 30.12.2011 again declared the allotment of Col. Hamid Hassan Khan as based on fraud and ordered for its resumption in favour of the state and its disposal through unrestricted open public auction. Hence, Writ Petition No.89- R/2012. 3. Further Mst. Shaheen Jadoon, granddaughter of Col. Hamid Hassan Khan, also filed a complaint viz Plot No.68 Ahmad Block Garden Town, Lahore, whereupon the Member (Judicial-V), Board of Revenue / Chief Settlement Commissioner / Administrator (Residual Properties) / Notified Officer, Punjab initiated inquiry proceedings and while hearing the concerned parties again vide order dated 13.03.2017 declared the alleged allotment in favour of the Col. (R) Hamid Hassan Khan as fraudulent, cancelled the same and also declared all the subsequent mutations / alienation of the said land as illegal and passed order to dispose of the property through unrestricted open public auction. Hence, Writ Petition No.12451/04/2017. 4. Writ Petition No.69194/2017 Mohsin Abbas Khan Vs.

Hence, Writ Petition No.12451/04/2017. 4. Writ Petition No.69194/2017 Mohsin Abbas Khan Vs. Member (Judicial-V) etc. Brief facts of the case, as stated in the writ petition, are that Col. (R) Hamid Hassan Khan, predecessor-in-interest of the petitioners, a displaced person having verified claim in Khata RL.II. No.42 allegedly got allotted land measuring 55 Kanal 16 Writ Petition No.12451/04/2017 4 Marla bearing Khasra Nos.5357, 5416, 5393, 5369, 5370, 5261, 5348, 5442/1, Moza Ichra, Lahore. The said land was acquired by Land Improvement Trust (now L.D.A.) for establishment of New Garden Town Scheme, Lahore. The petitioner purchased land measuring 05 Kanal 18 Marla and 126 Sq. Ft. in Khasra No.5348 from Col. Hamid Hassan Khan. That on the application of one Mst. Shaheen Jadoon the matter was probed by the Chief Settlement Commissioner who vide order dated 13.03.2017 declared the allotment of Col. (R) Hamid Hassan Khan as fraudulent and cancelled the same as well as all the subsequent alienations. 5. Writ Petition No.10498/2017 Arif Rehman Chughtai Vs. Member (Judicial-V) etc. Khan Abdul Rehman Chughtai, the predecessor of the petitioner, purchased land measuring 11 Kanal 06 Marla 06 Sq. Ft. situated Moza Ichra, Ferozpur Road, Lahore against total paid consideration of Rs.86,000/- from Col. (R) Hamid Hassan Khan and has also obtained declaration as exclusive owner of the aforesaid land through decree of the civil suit dated 13.12.1973. The Lahore Improvement Trust (Now LDA) acquired the aforesaid land in question for the establishment of New Garden Town Scheme and plot No.98 measuring 08 Kanal 13 Marla 68 Sq. Ft., in lieu of the aforesaid land, was exempted in favour of father of the petitioner. Further, the petitioner purchased plot No.97-B measuring 01 Kanal 07 Marla 42 Sq.

Ft., in lieu of the aforesaid land, was exempted in favour of father of the petitioner. Further, the petitioner purchased plot No.97-B measuring 01 Kanal 07 Marla 42 Sq. Ft. & Plot measuring 01 Kanal 18 Marla 50 Sq. Ft. (Total 3 Kanals 5 Marlas 92 Sq. Ft) from L.D.A against total paid consideration of Rs.89,87,927/-. Province of Punjab etc. Brief facts of this case, as stated in the writ petition, are that the petitioner is in possession of House No.80-B Garden Block, New Garden Town, Mouza Ichhra, Lahore; that the property in question is evacuee one and in possession of the petitioner since long. The respondents, instead of making allotment of the property in question in favour of the petitioner, passed impugned order dated 13.03.2017 for cancellation of allotment made in favour of the alleged allottee Col. (R) Hamid Hassan Khan. The petitioner, through this petition, has prayed for setting aside the impugned order dated 13.03.2017 and requested for sale of the plot in question to him as per market price. 7. Writ Petition No.12469/04/2017 President Association of Fatima Jinnah Old Graduate Vs. Assistant Commissioner etc. Brief facts of the case, as stated in the writ petition, are that the petitioner (Association of Fatima Jinnah Old Graduates) purchased Plot No.76-A measuring 02 Kanal, Garden Block, Garden Town Lahore from one Awais Sajjad through a registered sale deed dated 26.06.2014.

Assistant Commissioner etc. Brief facts of the case, as stated in the writ petition, are that the petitioner (Association of Fatima Jinnah Old Graduates) purchased Plot No.76-A measuring 02 Kanal, Garden Block, Garden Town Lahore from one Awais Sajjad through a registered sale deed dated 26.06.2014. Ft bearing Khasra No.5416, Khata RL.II. No.42 Moza Ichhra Lahore. The said land was acquired by the Lahore Improvement Trust (now L.D.A.) in 1967 and subsequently, a letter dated 23.01.1974 was issued to aforesaid vendees for exemption of land in question. Land measuring 02 Writ Petition No.12451/04/2017 6 Kanal 11 Marla 153 Sq. Ft was exempted on account of acquisition and remaining 01 Kanal 06 Marla 125 Sq. Ft. land was purchased by aforesaid persons for consideration of Rs.35126.43. Malik Liaqat Hussain and Nazeer-ud-Din executed sale deed dated 03.10.1977 in respect of the aforementioned land in favour of Mst. Riffat Nazir and Mst. Nargis Jehangir, who further sold the land to Dr. Muhammad Aslam through sale deed dated 24.06.1978. On 19.12.1993 the L.D.A. sub-divided the land in question into two portions i.e. Plot No.76 measuring 01 Kanal 19 Marla 51 Sq. Ft. and Plot No.76-A measuring 01 Kanal 19 Marla 02 Sq. Ft. (property in question). Dr. Muhammad Aslam sold the property in question to one Zaigham Sultan on 09.04.1994 who further alienated the same to Awais Sajjad, from whom the petitioner had purchased the same. The petitioner further contended that on 05.04.2017, he received a notice from the Assistant Commissioner/Collector, Tehsil Model Town, Lahore wherein he was asked to relieve the possession of the property in question i.e. Plot No.76-A on the ground that the allotment in favour of the alleged allottee Col. (R) Hamid Hassan Khan has been declared bogus and the subsequent transactions / alienations have also been set aside through order dated 13.03.2017 passed by the Chief Settlement Commissioner, Punjab.

Member (Judicial-V), Board of Revenue etc. Brief facts of the case, as stated in the writ petition, are that Shaikh Saleem Ali, real brother of the petitioner, was owner of plot No.88 Garden Block, New Garden Town, Lahore vide registered sale deed No.11205 dated 01.10.1963 and Transfer Letter dated 28.10.1996 was duly issued by L.D.A. Shaikh Saleem Ali died issueless on 9.11.2016 leaving behind the petitioner [Mst. Ijaz Khanum Aman Ullah] and respondent No.7 [Mst.

Shaikh Saleem Ali died issueless on 9.11.2016 leaving behind the petitioner [Mst. Ijaz Khanum Aman Ullah] and respondent No.7 [Mst. Iffat Rabia Hakeem, widow] as his legal heirs. Thereafter, the Chief Settlement Commissioner, Punjab vide order dated 13.03.2017 cancelled the allotment of Col. Member Board of Revenue etc. Brief facts of this case, as stated in the writ petition, are that the petitioners and their predecessors-in-interest had contended that they purchased the properties as mentioned hereunder from Col. (R) Hamid Hassan Khan: Writ Petition No.12451/04/2017 8 a. Father of the petitioner No.1 purchased land measuring 2 Kanals, bearing Khasra No.5393 vide registered sale deed No.11925 dated 29-12-1961. b. Ft. vide registered sale deed dated 02-07-1960 and then gifted the Petitioner No.4 & 5 their respective properties vide registered gift deeds dated 19-07-1987 and 20-08-1986. d. Petitioner No.5 was gifted land measuring 01 Kanal 12 Marla through registered gift deed dated 08-09-1977. e. Petitioner No.6 purchased land measuring 3 Kanals, 12 Marlas through registered sale deed dated 08-09-1977. The said plot was divided into two plots (plot no. 91-A and 91-B), the said division is evidenced vide letter dated 08-04-1982 issued by the Lahore Development Authority. The said plot was divided into two plots (plot no. 91-A and 91-B), the said division is evidenced vide letter dated 08-04-1982 issued by the Lahore Development Authority. The Chief Settlement Commissioner, Punjab vide order dated 13.03.2017 cancelled the entire alleged allotted land measuring 79 Kanal 04 Marla 63 Sq. Ft. of Col. (R) Hamid Hassan Khan and passed direction to dispose of the said land through unrestricted public auction. Hence, this writ petition. 11. Writ Petition No.13392/04/2017 Akhtar Begum Vs. Member Board of Revenue, Chief Settlement Commissioner etc. The petitioner purchased land measuring 05 Kanal 08 Marla 126 Sq. Ft. bearing Khasra No.5348 situated in Moza Ichra, Lahore from Col. (R) Hamid Hassan Khan, who was the alleged allottee of the said land. The said property was acquired by Lahore Improvement Trust (L.D.A) for establishment of New Garden Town Scheme, Lahore and the aforesaid land / plot stood exempted in favour of the petitioner. The petitioner after sanction of the site plan, constructed a house upon the land in question. Thereafter, the Chief Settlement Commissioner, Punjab vide order dated 13.03.2017 cancelled the allotment of Col. (R) Hamid Hassan Khan, from whom the petitioner had purchased the land in question. Hence, this writ petition. Writ Petition No.12451/04/2017 9 12. Writ Petition No.233137/2018 Dr. Ghulam Abbas Vs. The petitioner, through this petition, has prayed for setting aside the aforesaid order dated 13.03.2017. 13. Writ Petition No.58738/2019 Attique Ahmad Vs. Member Board of Revenue Brief facts of this case, as stated in the writ petition, are that Dr. Riaz Ahmad Shaikh, predecessor-in-interest of the petitioners, was owner of land measuring 03 Kanal 17 Marla bearing Khasra No.5351 Ichhra Lahore, who had purchased the same from Col. (R) Hamid Hassan Khan. Thereafter, the Chief Settlement Commissioner, Punjab vide order dated 13.03.2017 cancelled the allotment of Col. (R) Hamid Hassan Khan, from whom the predecessor of the petitioners had purchased the land in question hence the above petition. As no one has appeared on behalf of the petitioner as such it is being decided on the basis of available record. 14. The legal heirs of the claimant allottee Col. Hamid Hassan Khan as well as all subsequent purchasers from the said allottee Writ Petition No.12451/04/2017 10 question the validity of the impugned order dated 13.03.2017 on the ground that the allotment of Col. Hamid Hassan Khan fell under the past and closed transaction which cannot be reopened after 5 decades; that after repeal of the evacuee laws in 1975 the Notified Officer/ Chief Settlement Commissioner has no jurisdiction to probe into the matter which stay remote from the term “pending proceedings” as enunciated under Section 2 of the Evacuee Property and Displaced Persons Laws (Repeal) Act, 1975; that the subsequent purchasers have the protection of Section 41 of the Transfer of Property Act, 1882 as they have bonafidely purchased their respective properties/ plots etc. against paid consideration thus their transactions are protected under the law. 15.

Hamid Hassan Khan as well as all subsequent purchasers from the said allottee Writ Petition No.12451/04/2017 10 question the validity of the impugned order dated 13.03.2017 on the ground that the allotment of Col. Hamid Hassan Khan fell under the past and closed transaction which cannot be reopened after 5 decades; that after repeal of the evacuee laws in 1975 the Notified Officer/ Chief Settlement Commissioner has no jurisdiction to probe into the matter which stay remote from the term “pending proceedings” as enunciated under Section 2 of the Evacuee Property and Displaced Persons Laws (Repeal) Act, 1975; that the subsequent purchasers have the protection of Section 41 of the Transfer of Property Act, 1882 as they have bonafidely purchased their respective properties/ plots etc. against paid consideration thus their transactions are protected under the law. 15. The original record of the Settlement Department regarding the alleged allotment in favour of Col. (R) Hamid Hassan Khan was requisitioned on the application of one of the petitioners. The said record was examined by all the learned counsel for the parties in open Court in presence of Secretary (S&R), Board of Revenue. 16. I have heard the arguments of learned counsels for the parties and have gone through the record with their able assistance. I have also perused the written arguments submitted by the learned counsels for the petitioners. 17. The entire controversy revolves around that “as to whether Col. (R) Hamid Hassan Khan was a lawful allottee of the evacuee land measuring 79 Kanal 04 Marla 63 Sq. Ft against Khata RL-II No.42 in Mouza Icchra Lahore or otherwise”. After partition of sub-continent, Col. (R) Hamid Hassan Khan migrated from state of Patiala, India to the newly established state of Pakistan. He filed his claim on the basis of an alleged copy of registered sale deed as well as letter of Domadar Dass, Director of Rural Rehab:, Writ Petition No.12451/04/2017 11 Patiala before Claim Officer (Land), Lahore. On the above claim- application, the Deputy Claim Officer, Lahore passed following order on 23.09.1959: “IN THE COURT OF SH. ZAWAR AHMAD, CLAIM OFFICER (LAND), LAHORE. CASE No.373 Registration No.11990 Name and address Col. Sardar Hamid Hassan Khan Of the claimant S/O Sardar Zafar Ahmad Khan 3-Garden Town, Lahore. ORDER The claimant is present and has been heard. The claimant produced copy of registered sale deed E.X.P.C and copy of Letter of Domodar Dass, Director of Rural Rehab: Papsi, Patiala E.X.P.A.

ORDER The claimant is present and has been heard. The claimant produced copy of registered sale deed E.X.P.C and copy of Letter of Domodar Dass, Director of Rural Rehab: Papsi, Patiala E.X.P.A. According to the above sale deed E.X.P.C. and letter E.X.P.A. the claimant abandoned the following area within the urban limits of estate Patiala H.B..No.33. Nature of rights Chahi Total Ownership B. B. B.B. 30-3 30-3 (Thirty Bigha and three Biswa only) The claim is verified accordingly. Announced. Deputy Claim Officer 23/9/59 (Land) Central Record Office, Lahore.” But neither the original sale deed, mentioned in the aforesaid order, has been produced by claimant [Col. (R) Hamid Hassan Khan] nor the same is available on the record. The details of the sale deed i.e. sale deed number, volume number, date of sale deed, description of the property etc. has not been mentioned by the Deputy Claim Officer in the aforesaid order. The petitioners, being transferees from said alleged allottee, were under obligation to prove the validity of said alleged sale deed but no such original sale deed is produced before any competent forum. Thus, it can conveniently be held that no such sale deed was in existence but mere on the basis of copy of the alleged sale deed [which is also not available on record] or oral assertions, said Writ Petition No.12451/04/2017 12 claim of the alleged claimant [Col. (R) Hamid Hassan Khan] was allegedly got verified. 18. Thereafter, Col. (R) Hamid Hassan Khan approached the Claim Officer (Land), Central Record Office, West Pakistan, Lahore for change of category / classification of land from agricultural to garden land on the ground that the land abandoned by alleged allottee in Mouza Lehal & Patiala Khas, state of Patiala, India was of superior quality. On the basis of oral request of the claimant, the Claims Officer (Land) Central Record Officer, Pakistan, under Schedule IV changed the classification of land to that of superior quality (garden land) on 28.06.1961without obtaining specific information as prescribed under Schedule IV from the alleged allottee Col. (R) Hamid Hassan Khan and even no valid document in this regard is available on record.

Thereafter, Col. (R) Hamid Hassan Khan approached the Claim Officer (Land), Central Record Office, West Pakistan, Lahore for change of category / classification of land from agricultural to garden land on the ground that the land abandoned by alleged allottee in Mouza Lehal & Patiala Khas, state of Patiala, India was of superior quality. On the basis of oral request of the claimant, the Claims Officer (Land) Central Record Officer, Pakistan, under Schedule IV changed the classification of land to that of superior quality (garden land) on 28.06.1961without obtaining specific information as prescribed under Schedule IV from the alleged allottee Col. (R) Hamid Hassan Khan and even no valid document in this regard is available on record. Thus in the absence of any reliable trustworthy evidence-information, the classification of land could not be changed. For reference, Schedule IV of The Registration of Claims (Displaced Persons) Rules, 1955 promulgated on 01.04.1955 and gazetted on 08.04.1955, is reproduced as under: SCHEDULE IV FOR AGRICULTURAL LAND AND GARDENS IN URBAN AREAS Situation: (a) Name of urban area where property lies (state whether a Corporation, a Municipal Committee, a Notified Area Committee, a Town Area Committee, a Small Town Committee, a Sanitary Committee or a Cantonment). (b) Police Station (c) Tahsil/Taluka. (d) District (e) Province/State. I. Land 1. Survey numbers, with area of each survey number. 2. Classification: (a) Cultivable. Writ Petition No.12451/04/2017 13 (b) Uncultivable 3. Whether land was: (a) flow irrigated, (b) lift irrigated, (c) well-irrigated (d) canal irrigated, or (e) barani. 4. Extent of applicant‟s interest in the property. State the names and addresses of co-sharers, if any, their relationship with the applicant and the extent of share of each. 5. Land revenue or rent assessed on the end. II. Garden and garden land. 1. Area: Survey numbers with area of each survey number. 2. Approximate number of plaints under each variety. 3. Nature of irrigation, if any. 4. Extent of applicant‟s interest in the property. State names and addresses of co-sharers, if any, their relationship with the applicant and the extent of share of each. Land revenue or rent assessed on the land. 5. Nature of applicant‟s interest, whether proprietor, tenant, etc. State exact nature of tenancy. All the aforesaid proceedings were conducted by the Settlement Authorities on the oral assertions of the Col. (R) Hamid Hassan Khan and without the basis of any valid document, category of the land was changed.

State exact nature of tenancy. All the aforesaid proceedings were conducted by the Settlement Authorities on the oral assertions of the Col. (R) Hamid Hassan Khan and without the basis of any valid document, category of the land was changed. Furthermore, there is tempering in the order dated 28.06.1961 including the change of the date as 27.06.1961 to 28.06.1961 which also shakes authenticity of the said order. Thereafter, on 03.07.1961, Col. (R) Hamid Hassan Khan filed a Form UR-1 and on the basis of the said order, entitlement certificate was issued on 08.07.1961. Further, as per record neither any order for allotment of the land in question ever passed by the competent authority is available on record in favour of alleged allottee nor any allotment order is furnished or referred by the petitioners before this Court. Writ Petition No.12451/04/2017 14 Moreover the original record of Central Record Room as well as the record of Mouza Lehal, Mouza Patiala Khas, State of Patiala India received to Settlement Department, Pakistan, was requisitioned and perusal whereof shows that Col. (R) Hamid Hassan Khan was not found owner of a single inch of land in the aforesaid Mouzas of state of Patiala. Even the petitioners have not placed on record any valid document to show that Col. (R) Hamid Hassan Khan was owner of land in Mouza Lehal and Patiala Khas, State of Patiala India. The above record has not been controverted by the petitioners through any trustworthy documentary evidence, as such, the entire Claim filed by him was based on fraud but despite that the alleged claimant fraudulently obtained entitlement certificate and got entered his name in the revenue record without any valid order of the competent authority. 19. Further as per the revenue record of Moza Ichhra, Lahore pertaining to years 1953-1954, the land in question was shown as evacuee and the name of Col. (R) Hamid Hassan Khan is mentioned in the column of cultivator as “Rehabilitation Commissioner care of Col.

The above record has not been controverted by the petitioners through any trustworthy documentary evidence, as such, the entire Claim filed by him was based on fraud but despite that the alleged claimant fraudulently obtained entitlement certificate and got entered his name in the revenue record without any valid order of the competent authority. 19. Further as per the revenue record of Moza Ichhra, Lahore pertaining to years 1953-1954, the land in question was shown as evacuee and the name of Col. (R) Hamid Hassan Khan is mentioned in the column of cultivator as “Rehabilitation Commissioner care of Col. Sardar Hamid Hassan Khan”. As per the subsequent entries in the Revenue record the name of Col. (R) Hamid Hassan Khan was recorded as Tayyandar in column of cultivation and in the column of ownership, the Central Government is shown as owner of the land which (land) was further devolved upon the Provincial Government. Furthermore, there is nothing on record to show that on which basis, the name of Col. (R) Hamid Hassan Khan was incorporated in the revenue record as Tayyandar. Mere an unauthorized entry in the revenue record showing the claimant as Tayyandar cannot be termed as valid allotment of the land, as such the above Tayyandar had no allotment order in his favour and was denuded of any valid title over the land in question. Even otherwise, the claimant asserted his Writ Petition No.12451/04/2017 15 ownership on the basis of alleged copy of sale deed in his favour but subsequently by taking somersault he got his status determined as Tayyandar, whereas in presence of the record received from India, which demonstrate the non-ownership of the claimant, no question of determination of the alleged claimant as Tayyandar arises. Further in the Register of Haqdaran Zameen, the status of Col. (R) Hamid Hassan Khan was changed from Tayyandar to „owner‟ without any valid order by the Settlement Authorities. Much emphasis is laid on the lack of jurisdiction of the Chief Settlement Commissioner / Notified Officer to probe any matter of allotment after repeal of the evacuee law in 1974, suffice it to say that it has already been observed in the preceding paragraph that the alleged allotment is based on manifest fraud and forgery and it is settled law that any evacuee land obtained by committing misrepresentation of the facts and fraud in that Writ Petition No.12451/04/2017 16 eventuality, the Chief Settlement Commissioner, being successor of Custodian of the evacuee land / property and Claims Officer, has jurisdiction to initiate inquiry or take cognizance of the matter regarding transfer of the evacuee land. This issue of jurisdiction of the Chief Settlement Commissioner/Notified Officer has been settled by the Hon‟ble Supreme Court of Pakistan in a judgment reported as Messrs Beach Luxury Hotels, Karachi Vs. Messrs Anas Muneer Ltd and others (2016 SCMR 222) wherein it is observed that when a matter of alienation of evacuee land is re-opened, the Settlement Authority has the jurisdiction to re-examine all the facts pertaining to the title of the parties from the very inception of claim and to decide the matter according to available record as per law.

This issue of jurisdiction of the Chief Settlement Commissioner/Notified Officer has been settled by the Hon‟ble Supreme Court of Pakistan in a judgment reported as Messrs Beach Luxury Hotels, Karachi Vs. Messrs Anas Muneer Ltd and others (2016 SCMR 222) wherein it is observed that when a matter of alienation of evacuee land is re-opened, the Settlement Authority has the jurisdiction to re-examine all the facts pertaining to the title of the parties from the very inception of claim and to decide the matter according to available record as per law. In another judgment cited as Virasat Ullah Versus Bashir Ahmad, Settlement Commissioner (Industries) and another (1969 SCMR 154), the Hon'ble Supreme Court of Pakistan has observed as under:- “We are unable to see any point in this leave petition except the uneasiness on the part of the petitioner to undo the notice issued to him by the Settlement Commissioner which merely required that the petitioner should disclose his credential as the transferee of the property in dispute. The leaned Single Judge has directed that the entitlement of the petitioner should be duly examined and we see no apparent reason why the petitioner wants to avoid this examination. The grievance of the petitioner at this stage is baseless and premature. This petition is dismissed.” (emphasis supplied) The above said view has been reiterated in case reported as Chief Settlement Commissioner, Lahore V. Raja Muhammad Fazil Khan and others (PLD 1975 SC 331) and Muhammad Siddique Vs M.B.R/C.S.C, Punjab and others (2017 MLD 572). Further Section 21 of the General Clauses Act, 1897 confers an inherent jurisdiction to an authority or its successor authority to reverse its earlier erroneous or illegal order. Similarly if any benefit has been obtained from authority by practicing misrepresentation or fraud, the same forum is vested with inbuilt inherent jurisdiction Writ Petition No.12451/04/2017 17 to undo its wrong. In this regard the Hon‟ble Supreme Court of Pakistan in a case titled as Muhammad Baran and others Vs. Member (Settlement and Rehabilitation) Board of Revenue Punjab and others (PLD 1991 SC 691) has held that if any order is obtained from the authorities through misrepresentation, fraud and forgery, in that eventuality the same authority can undo such illegal order either on its own motion or on the information received to it through any application.

In the case of obtaining evacuee land through fraud, the settlement authority may reverse such allotment order and in such like matter, the superior Courts can withheld the exercise of their discretionary writ jurisdiction to annul the order of authority, even though it was clearly without jurisdiction. Relevant portion of the judgment (supra) is reproduced as under:- “Putting this observation in juxtaposition to the present case; if the allotments relied upon by the appellants made by the Settlement functionaries were illegal and without jurisdiction and indeed if they were also based on fraud and forgery, in that eventuality even if the Board of Revenue which exposed fraud and forgery and set aside illegal transfer of properties worth millions by its own order, the High Court would not in exercise of its discretionary (Writ ) jurisdiction annul the order of the Board of Revenue, even though, to borrow the language used in the case of Raunaq Ali the latter “was clearly without jurisdiction.” Further, in another judgment reported as Shamrooz Khan Vs. For reference, the relevant portion of the affidavit is reproduced as under: Writ Petition No.12451/04/2017 29 "4۔ ہی ہک نم محلف حہبق دعت ری1 انکل8 رمےل رسخہ ربمن5348 ومعض رھچہ باہق لیصحت ٹنیک علض الوہح ابر یگیئ تمیق دقن وج افلض دع تل نیعت لنے رخدی لنےن وک ایتح وہں دح افلض دع تل یک اجبن ےس ری یئگ تلہم ںیم حمق زخ ہن رساکح ںیم عمج لن رداگن۔" In this regard it is observed that the above request is untenable, as the said evacuee property is situated in the heart of the Lahore City and the superior Courts of this country have settled this issue that the evacuee property, either urban or agricultural, can only be disposed of by the authorities through unrestricted open public auction. A learned Division Bench of this Court has resolved this issue in a latest judgment cited as Malik Allah Ditta and others Vs. Member, Board of Revenue/(Judicial-V)/ Chief Settlement Commissioner / Notified Officer, Punjab, Lahore and another (2022 CLC 414) wherein it is held that the Notified Officer / Chief Settlement Commissioner has no jurisdiction to alienate the evacuee land through any private treaty and the only mode for disposal of the state assets is to put the same to unrestricted transparent open public auction.

A learned Division Bench of this Court has resolved this issue in a latest judgment cited as Malik Allah Ditta and others Vs. Member, Board of Revenue/(Judicial-V)/ Chief Settlement Commissioner / Notified Officer, Punjab, Lahore and another (2022 CLC 414) wherein it is held that the Notified Officer / Chief Settlement Commissioner has no jurisdiction to alienate the evacuee land through any private treaty and the only mode for disposal of the state assets is to put the same to unrestricted transparent open public auction. The relevant portion of the judgment (supra) is as under:- “6. Now adverting to the second question i.e. whether the Notified Officer has the jurisdiction to sell the evacuee land through private treaty”. Admittedly after the repeal of evacuee laws with effect from 01.07.1974 by promulgation of Evacuee Property and Displaced Persons Laws (Repeal) Act, 1975 all the evacuee land under Section 3 of the Act ibid by operation of law stood vest with the provincial government against the paid consideration thus the said land has attained the status of public property and its disposal has to be made as prescribed under the law, scheme policy on the subject and it is settled that when law requires a thing/act to be done in a particular manner that must be done according to the described modus operandi otherwise it wears no sanctity, effectiveness in the eyes of law. Admittedly the jurisdiction of the Notified Officer has been restricted to the pending proceedings as envisaged under Section 2 of the Act, 1975 and he has no unlimited power rather he had to exercise its jurisdiction with the precincts prescribed under the law regulations, rules, policies and instructions on the subject. Any unwarranted act of a state functionary is liable to be set at naught without any hesitation. Undoubtedly the public functionaries are the Writ Petition No.12451/04/2017 30 ostensible custodians of the state assets and they cannot be allowed to dole the state assets upon their cherished / blue eyed persons at their own whims and fancies. Undeniably the sacred public assets can only be disposed of in extraordinary transparent manner by leaving nil space for doubts and in this regard the most transparent and suitable modus operandi which better serves the public cause is putting such disposable property to unrestricted public auction in order to fetch befitting better price.

The object of the above mode is to eliminate the nefarious elements of favoritism, nepotism and the corrupt practices as well as to provide a fair equitable opportunity to every citizen of Pakistan to take advantage from public assets / opportunities and to improve his financial and social status. The Hon'ble Supreme Court of Pakistan in a case titled as Muhammad Ramzan & Others Vs. Member (Revenue) Chief Settlement Commissioner & Others (1997 SCMR 1635) while deciding the mode of disposal of available evacuee properties, held that “... it should be disposed of through unrestricted public auction”. The above celebrated principal has been reiterated in case cited as American International School System Vs. Mian Muhammad Ramzan & Others (2015 SCMR 1449) wherein the Hon‟ble Supreme Court of Pakistan has manifestly settled the procedure for disposal of the state assets through transparent, unrestricted open public auction. It is true that we have never leaned in favour of giving of preferential treatment to the Government departments or agencies but then we are equally obliged, while granting relief, to ensure that public interest is not permitted to be jeopardized and public property is not allowed to be squandered through mere collusion of some representative of a Government agency”. (emphasis supplied) 9. Moreover in the plethora of the hallmark dicta of the Hon‟ble Apex Court of the country it is successively held that the state assets must be disposed of in fair transparent, universally recognized modus operandi of public auction, thus all the organs of the state are placed under mandatory obligation to comply with the orders of the Hon‟ble Supreme Court of Pakistan as enshrined in Article 189 of the Constitution of the Islamic Republic of Pakistan and any deviation whereof that would indeed be nullity void ab initio in the eyes of law. Reliance in this regard is placed on the cases cited as Muhammad Sharif Vs. Settlement Commissioner and others (2007 SCMR 707), Iffat Jabeen Vs. District Education Officer (M.E.E), Lahore and another (2011 SCMR 437) and Dr. Iqrar Ahmad Khan Vs. Dr. Muhammad Ashraf and others (2021 SCMR 1509). As such it is crystal clear from the aforementioned discussion that the Notified Officer / Chief Settlement Commissioner has no jurisdiction to sell the evacuee land through private treaty and the only mode for disposal of the state assets is to put the same to unrestricted transparent open public auction but the Chief Settlement Commissioner while deciding the application in violation of above consistent law allowed the appellant to purchase the land on market price which in itself is a kind of private treaty, thus these findings being contrary to law are liable to be set aside.” 24.

As regard the contention of the petitioner Arif Rehman Chughtai (W.P.No.10498/2017) that his father has purchased land measuring 11 Kanal 06 Marla 06 Sq. Ft. New Garden Town, Moza Ichhra, Ferozpur Road, Lahore from Col. (R) Hamid Hassan Khan against consideration through decree of civil court Writ Petition No.12451/04/2017 33 dated 13.12.1973 and in lieu of aforesaid land, a plot No.98 measuring 08 Kanal 13 Marla 68 Sq. Ft was exempted in his favour, suffice it to say that as the land in question is “evacuee” one, thus as per law, the Civil Court has no jurisdiction to intrude into the jurisdictional realm of the settlement department under Section 41 of the Pakistan Administration of Evacuee Property Act, 1957 and Sections 22 & 25 of Displaced Persons (Land Settlement) Act, 1958 and even if any decree passed by the Civil Court, that would be without jurisdiction and nullity in the eyes of law or void ab-initio and same is inexecutable. Reliance is placed on the case titled as Muhammad Sadiq (decd.) through L.Rs & Others Vs Mushtaq & Others (2011 SCMR 239) and Allah Rakha (deceased) through LRs and others Vs. Additional Commissioner (Revenue) Gujranwala and others (2020 SCMR 502). So far as Arif Rehman Chughtai‟s claim that he had purchased plot No.97-B New Garden Town Scheme, Lahore measuring 01 Kanal 07 Marla & 42 Sq. Ft bearing Khasra No.5374 from L.D.A. (the acquiring agency of the land) after making payment of full consideration amount. As the aforesaid Khasra No.5374 was comprising on the evacuee land, as such, the L.D.A. was not competent to alienate / sale the aforesaid land to any person as per the principle laid down by the Hon'ble Supreme Court of Pakistan in a judgment cited as American International School System Vs.
